title: Introduction and Background
objective: Introduce the research context of pedestrian interaction with vehicles at crosswalks, establish the importance of understanding pedestrian crossing behavior, and outline the scope of this comparative review

title: Methodological Approaches in Pedestrian Crossing Research
objective: Survey and categorize the methodological families used in the literature, including virtual reality experiments, regression-based modeling, and discrete choice models

title: External Vehicle Communication Features for Autonomous Vehicles
objective: Synthesize evidence on pedestrian preferences for external features on fully autonomous vehicles, including visual interfaces (walking silhouette, braking text, smiley face) and audible signals

title: Pedestrian Red-Light Running Behavior at Signalized Intersections
objective: Analyze factors influencing pedestrian red-light running propensity, including personal characteristics (gender, age, trip purpose) and environmental factors (signal timing, traffic conditions)

title: Comparative Analysis and Claim Reconciliation
objective: Identify and analyze claim differences across papers addressing related pedestrian crossing tasks, highlighting contradictions in findings and proposing clearer comparison criteria

title: Research Gaps and Future Directions
objective: Synthesize the identified research gaps from the comparison analysis and propose directions for future research to resolve discrepancies and advance understanding of pedestrian-vehicle interaction
